Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Audience: IT / Service Desk; Developers; System/Data Owners
Learning Outcome: Describe the discovery methods for each asset type: MDM/EDR for endpoints, SBOMs + SCA/SAST/DAST for custom software, vendor security advisories for SaaS dependencies, and approved lists for browser/IDE/SaaS extensions

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Audience: IT / Service Desk; Developers; System/Data Owners
Learning Outcome: Prioritize vulnerabilities using severity and exploitability signals, then incorporate service criticality and data sensitivity; confirm applicability before assigning remediation targets

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Audience: IT / Service Desk; Developers; System/Data Owners
Learning Outcome: State the remediation timelines: Critical (15 days), High (30 days), Medium (60 days), Low (best effort); explain that timelines may tighten during active exploitation and that exceptions require CISO approval

Module: F - Governance, risk, and compliance awareness
Learning Outcome: Know that the Vulnerability and Patch Management Policy exists and is managed by IT; anti-malware and patching are enforced automatically via MDM; if you notice unusual system behavior or a suspected vulnerability, raise a flag in Slack #general, DM an IT team member or your manager, or email security@civicactions.com

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Learning Outcome: Use staged deployment groups in MDM (alpha, beta, company-wide) for endpoint patches; treat patch waves as Standard changes within maintenance windows; validate SaaS configuration changes in test/staging tenants where available

Training Name: Training 3 — IT Operations: Change, Configuration & Patch Management
Audience: IT / Service Desk; Developers; System/Data Owners
Learning Outcome: Verify fixes through re-scan or functional validation; attach evidence to the ticket; maintain reports and tickets as Controlled Records
